comment.body:
Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.
This is a mental trap. I tell you what I mean by that.
You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...

Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.

You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...
